steps.title: Express the four consecutive odd integers
steps.content:
Starting with the first odd integer $x$, the next consecutive odd integers increase by 2 each time:
  • First: $x$
  • Second: $x + 2$
  • Third: $x + 4$
  • Fourth: $x + 6$

steps.title: Convert the word condition into an inequality
steps.content:
The phrase product of 24 and the third is at most 42 less than the sum of the first and fourth translates to:
$$24(x + 4) \leq (x + (x + 6)) - 42$$
Simplify the right side:
$$24(x + 4) \leq 2x + 6 - 42$$
$$24(x + 4) \leq 2x - 36$$

steps.title: Expand and solve for x
steps.content:
Expand the left side:
$$24x + 96 \leq 2x - 36$$
Subtract $2x$ from both sides:
$$22x + 96 \leq -36$$
Subtract 96 from both sides:
$$22x \leq -132$$
Divide by 22:
$$x \leq -6$$

steps.title: Find the greatest possible odd integer value
steps.content: We need the greatest value of $x$ such that $x \leq -6$ and $x$ is odd. Checking odd integers near the boundary: $-5 \gt -6$ (violates the constraint) and $-7 \lt -6$ (satisfies the constraint and is odd). The greatest odd integer satisfying $x \leq -6$ is $x = -7$. The answer is -7.
